reasoning: Pixel art structure is generally sound — discrete pixels visible at 8x zoom, dark outline mostly consistent at 1px equivalent, background clean and low-noise. However three tells raise concern: (1) The white chest/belly region shows a radial gradient glow effect transitioning smoothly from bright center to darker edges rather than the 2-3 discrete hard-tone pixel clusters expected in clean pixel art — this is the strongest AI tell. (2) The orange body shading transitions across the torso feel slightly smooth, with more intermediate tones than a hand-authored palette would typically use. (3) The leg/appendage pairs show minor left-right asymmetry in pixel placement at 8x zoom (left rear leg cluster differs from right). Sub-pixel features absent as expected for pixel art. No severe color bleed, no melting details, no semantic failures — the spider/crab enemy reads correctly. The gradient belly shading is the key strike; everything else is borderline. Marginal rather than fail because the pixel grid is respected and no single artifact is egregious.
